current_html:
§ 1600.220 What contracts and subcontracts are covered transactions?

First-tier procurements (i.e., primary contracts) under a covered nonprocurement transaction are covered transactions. Although the OMB guidance at 2 CFR 180.220(c) allows a Federal agency to do so (see also optional lower tier coverage in the figure in the appendix to 2 CFR part 180), DFC does not extend coverage of nonprocurement suspension and debarment requirements beyond first-tier procurement under a covered nonprocurement transaction. Moreover, for purposes of determining whether a procurement contract is included as a covered transaction, the threshold in 2 CFR 180.220(b) is increased from $25,000 to the “simplified acquisition threshold” as defined in 48 CFR 2.101.
proposed_html:
§ 1600.220
What contracts and subcontracts are covered transactions?

First-tier procurements ( i.e., primary contracts) under a covered nonprocurement transaction are covered transactions. Although the OMB regulation at 2 CFR 180.220(c) allows a Federal agency to do so (see also optional lower tier coverage in the figure in appendix A to 2 CFR part 180), DFC does not extend coverage of nonprocurement suspension and debarment requirements beyond first-tier procurement under a covered nonprocurement transaction. Moreover, for purposes of determining whether a procurement contract is included as a covered transaction, the threshold in 2 CFR 180.220(b) is increased from $25,000 to the "simplified acquisition threshold" as defined in 48 CFR 2.101.
